teamObjective.explanation: I am in an endgame race with only two cards left. The fastest path to going out is to seize the lead and then play my remaining card.

partnerBelief.summary: Partner likely has several cards remaining and may be able to support later, but no specific cards are known.
evidence: publicHistory only contains new_game, so no reliable signals yet

opponentBelief.summary: Opponents may hold trump cards, higher non-trump singles, or bombs, but exact holdings are unknown.
evidence: handCounts show each opponent has 3 cards
trumpRank is 7; no trump cards have been played yet

actionRationale.primaryReason: Leading the Ace of spades maximizes the chance of winning this trick. If it wins, I retain the lead and can then play the 2 to go out. The Ace is the strongest non-trump single in my hand and beats any non-trump single except trumps.

whyNotAlternatives.reason: The 2 is much weaker and more likely to be beaten by an opponent, which would cost me the lead and make it harder to finish my hand.

risks: An opponent may play a trump (rank 7 or joker) over the Ace and take the lead.
If the Ace loses, I may never regain the lead with only a 2 remaining.
riskAssessment.mitigation: There is no safer lead available; playing the Ace first is the highest-probability way to win the current trick and control the next one.
